description: Design scenario eval packs for active design capability proof. This is scaffolding for A029-A034 and is not a validator schema.

notAccepted: browser proof for non-UI-only copy/data tasks unless they emit UI
receipts: runtime-issued workflow receipt for every claimed specialist producer, reviewer, validator, or external tool invocation
hostInvocation.source and invocationId bound to durable outputs
scenario owner receipt separate from command or skill receipt when a named design agent owns the scenario
retrieval: project memory decision or explicit no-prior-memory reason
Code RAG citation or explicit non-code-artifact reason
CodeGraph symbol evidence when the scenario changes runtime code, agent wiring, or validator behavior
browserRuntimeProof: desktop and mobile viewport screenshots for UI-emitting scenarios
console and network diagnostics with secrets, cookies, localStorage, tokens, and private URLs excluded
interaction evidence for controls, navigation, animation, chart drilldown, or deck navigation when present
reduced-motion or non-motion fallback evidence for motion-heavy artifacts
privacy: no raw provider config, tokens, cookies, localStorage, secrets, or private screenshots in public artifacts
sensitive browser captures must be private/non-committable and cannot satisfy public release evidence

scenarios.intent: Prove a web product prototype from brief through visual direction, responsive UI, runtime proof, and polish review.
evidenceRequirements: brief interpretation and target=web resolution
design-intelligence source packet with product, style, color, typography, UX, landing, app-interface, and creative-pack evidence
design diversity evidence when alternatives are shown
prototype capability plan if advanced interaction, charts, media, or dependency use is proposed
artifact links for direction, tokens or local CSS variables, prototype files, and review notes
scenarios.acceptanceSignal: A reviewer can trace the web artifact from brief to source evidence to runtime proof without relying on global maturity.

scenarios.intent: Prove deck design capability despite no dedicated deck agent in the current agents/_design tree.
evidenceRequirements: audience, narrative arc, slide count, and decision objective
brand/token reuse or documented deck-specific extension
slide-level hierarchy, chart/copy notes, and speaker-context evidence
export or preview artifact link when runtime deck tooling exists
documented absence of dedicated presentation-deck agents if still true at execution time

runtimeBrowserProof.condition: required when deck is emitted as HTML preview or interactive presentation; not required for outline-only copy proof
interactions: chart or build step when present

scenarios.acceptanceSignal: Deck proof is scenario-based and receipt-bound, not inferred from removed or missing presentation-specific agents.

scenarios.intent: Prove mobile-native UI judgment for a task-heavy flow with platform constraints and accessibility.
evidenceRequirements: mobile-native target resolution and platform assumptions
touch target, safe-area, keyboard, permission, loading, empty, and error state plan
source packet with mobile-native creative pack, app-interface, typography, icons, and accessibility evidence
prototype or handoff artifact with iOS/Android distinctions when applicable
scenarios.acceptanceSignal: Mobile capability is proven by platform-aware artifacts and runtime viewport evidence.

scenarios.intent: Prove desktop app UI capability for dense operational workflows in Tauri or Electron shells.
evidenceRequirements: desktop viewport policy and shell target selection
menu, command palette, window chrome, density, resize, empty/error/loading state plan
source packet with app-interface, stack guidance, interaction patterns, and platform standards
artifact links for desktop shell mock, states, and reviewer notes

diagnostics: network when app preview loads remote assets

scenarios.acceptanceSignal: Desktop proof handles shell constraints instead of presenting a generic web dashboard.

scenarios.intent: Prove design-system architecture from brand direction to tokens, components, states, and implementation handoff.
evidenceRequirements: approved or proposed brand direction with token rationale
component catalog scope and ownership contract
color, typography, spacing, motion, icon, and state token evidence
contrast, focus, disabled, error, empty, loading, and density states
handoff path to component library or custom implementation

runtimeBrowserProof.condition: required when a component specimen, styleboard, or prototype preview is emitted; not required for pure contract inventory
interactions: theme or density toggle when present

scenarios.acceptanceSignal: The system proof names implementable tokens and component states, not only visual adjectives.

scenarios.intent: Prove accessibility review capability with actionable remediation for UI artifacts.
evidenceRequirements: WCAG-oriented review checklist scoped to artifact type
keyboard path, focus order, landmark, label, contrast, motion, and error-message evidence
screen-size and zoom risk notes
remediation plan with owner and acceptance criteria

diagnostics: accessibility tree or DOM evidence when available

scenarios.acceptanceSignal: Accessibility proof is artifact-specific and includes fixes, not just a checklist pass claim.

scenarios.intent: Prove chart and dense data interface capability using synthetic data and chart-specific accessibility.
evidenceRequirements: synthetic dataset contract with edge cases
chart selection rationale and rejected chart alternatives
axis, scale, annotation, tooltip, legend, empty, loading, and error state plan
table or text fallback for chart meaning
color semantics and contrast evidence
scenarios.acceptanceSignal: Data visualization proof shows decision accuracy, accessibility, and responsive behavior with synthetic data.

scenarios.intent: Prove UX/product copy capability for voice, conversion, empty/error states, and regulated-risk wording.
evidenceRequirements: audience, intent, voice, trust burden, and regulated-risk classification
headline, CTA, onboarding, empty, error, success, disclosure, and microcopy variants
plain-language and accessibility review notes
approved/rejected wording rationale and source packet when factual claims are used

runtimeBrowserProof.condition: required when copy is placed into a UI artifact; not required for standalone copy deck or voice matrix
interactions: form error or CTA path when UI-bound

scenarios.acceptanceSignal: Copy proof distinguishes standalone language quality from UI-bound runtime readability.

agentCoverage.exemption: A028 required surfaces do not include browser-extension UI. Extension proof should be added as a separate scenario if A029-A034 route a design target to extension.
